g.text: · private PDF research, on your machine
A graduate-level textbook on cloud computing, covering virtualization, containers,
orchestration, and model deployment on shared infrastructure.
From the cloud computing textbook, list the main stages of the ML model deployment lifecycle.
From the textbook, the ML model deployment lifecycle covers four main stages:
1. Training. Fit the model on the labelled corpus (Chapter 4, p.12).
2. Packaging. Containerise the model, weights, and env manifest (Chapter 4, p.14).
3. Deployment. Push to a serving runtime behind an API endpoint (Chapter 5, p.18).
4. Monitoring. Track traffic, drift, and errors in production (Chapter 5, p.22).
A fifth, ongoing stage is retraining once production data drifts from the training
distribution — the textbook calls this continuous training (p.24).▌
